title: The Illusion of a 'Perfect' Model
description: A diagram illustrating the relationship between model complexity, error, and the concepts of overfitting and underfitting, culminating in the ideal 'sweet spot' that is often mistakenly thought of as 'perfect'.
relationships: As model complexity increases, training error continuously decreases
Generalization error decreases to a minimum point (the sweet spot) and then increases again due to overfitting
The 'Perfect' model is an idealized goal at the minimum point of the generalization error curve, representing the best possible trade-off between bias and variance
